facade_description: 198 Augusta Ave presents a converted house-form, detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, detached typology. The facade reads primarily as stucco, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 1, 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles.
